title: Overprovision Node Capacity For A Cluster — Calculate the total reserved resources
text:
For example, with 5 replicas each reserving 0.1 CPU and 200MiB of memory:
Total CPU reserved: 5 × 0.1 = 0.5 (in the Pod specification, you'll write the quantity
500m
)
Total memory reserved: 5 × 200MiB = 1GiB (in the Pod specification, you'll write
1 Gi
)
To scale the Deployment, adjust the number of replicas based on your cluster's size and expected workload:
kubectl scale deployment capacity-reservation --replicas
=
5
Verify the scaling:
kubectl get deployment capacity-reservation
The output should reflect the updated number of replicas:
NAME                   READY   UP-TO-DATE   AVAILABLE   AGE
capacity-reservation   5/5     5            5           2m
